title: A reliability update for customers in New Orleans East
summary: Entergy New Orleans continues to make progress on reliability improvements in the Kenilworth area of New Orleans East. This work is focused on strengthening the underground electric system by replacing critical equipment that will help improve reliability and reduce the likelihood of future outages.

title: Team brings quantum computing into FLiBe chemistry calculations
summary: Researchers have reported using a combination of quantum and classical computing to calculate chemistry connected to tritium speciation in a FLiBe—lithium fluoride and beryllium fluoride—molten salt blanket, demonstrating a promising direction for unlocking tritium breeding in fusion machines.

title: Entergy New Orleans hosts annual Energize NOLA! Community Fair and Back-to-School Giveaway
summary: Entergy New Orleans is inviting families across the city to join us for the annual Energize NOLA! Community Fair and Back-to-School Giveaway on Saturday, July 18, at the Joe W. Brown Memorial Recreation Center from 11 a.m. to 2 p.m.

title: ANS webinar looks to Utah’s nuclear future
summary: On July 9, the American Nuclear Society hosted the third installment of its “Nuclear in the States” webinar series. This time, the webinar looked to the current landscape of Utah’s nuclear sector—from its policy developments to ongoing projects to workforce needs. ANS CEO Craig Piercy, who led the discussion, was joined by Chris Hayter, cofounder of Hi Tech Solutions and founder of Fulcrum Point Energy; Emy Lesofski, Utah Gov. Spencer Cox’s energy advisor and director of the Utah Office of Energy Development (OED); and Jeff Richardson, president of D&D and chief projects officer at EnergySolutions.

title: Terms finalized for Australian uranium exports to India
summary: Following up on an agreement that was signed 12 years ago, Australia and India have finalized the details by which Australian uranium will be exported to India for peaceful purposes and under the safeguards of the International Atomic Energy Agency. The Administrative Arrangement to enable the long-term exports was reached at the Third India–Australia Annual Summit, held in Melbourne on July 9. The summit coincided with a meeting between Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.

title: Summer 2026 update on regional transmission investment now available; 3 projects under construction in New England
summary: One upgrade has been placed in service since March 2026, according to the latest update to the Regional System Plan. The post Summer 2026 update on regional transmission investment now available; 3 projects under construction in New England appeared first on ISO Newswire.

title: Global liquefied natural gas trade volumes reached record high in 2025
summary: EIA says global LNG trade hit a record in 2025 and U.S. LNG exports rose sharply, supporting LNG infrastructure and gas-exposed assets, while Strait of Hormuz disruptions and sanctions still create supply-chain risk.
claims: Global LNG trade reached a record 56.3 Bcf/d in 2025.
U.S. LNG exports rose 26% to 15.1 Bcf/d and EIA says 2026 flows were slowed by Hormuz-related disruption.

title: Meet the pros: Spencer Smith
summary: At Entergy, every employee has a designated role during a storm response, and whether it’s a minor disruption or a major event, each role is critical to helping us restore power as safely and as quickly as possible.
